Like, you know what? I always forget myself sometimes. I forget because they're just so natural to me about the impact that my tonality has on people. I almost forget it sometimes because I just take it for granted. My tonality is unconscious. My mind always applies the right tonality at the exact right moment. It's only when I hear myself, when I watch tapes of myself and I'm like, damn, I'm like, I'm like that was some powerful stuff that I start to realize. Just remember this. I'm going to ask you to cue me today. I want you to tape yourself. I want you to do me a favor. I want you to tape yourself at some point today. Just spend like, you know, one, one, let's say if you're on the phone selling, just get them taped out the client. Just your end. Tape the first three or four minutes of a phone call today at some point. And I want you to tape that call and be absolutely conscious of your tonality. I want you to tape the call with tonality being on your mind. I'm just going to focus on my tonality. Every getting every word right. Every beat right. Just focus on that and let everything else happen the way it normally happens. I want you to just do it. Just one little period today. Just for, excuse me, do this. All right. And then don't do anything with that. And then tomorrow I want you to tape another call. Same two or three days. Just take a short period, two or three minutes of this, the same part of the sale. There's like this much of a difference in your tone of voice, but the impact on the other person is just immense. Never forget that. I've said to you many times, you know, the tonality we use are the secret weapons of influence. Tonality is a weapon and some level makes seem like it's a bad, I mean a weapon for bad. This is a weapon for good. Your tonality, what it's basically doing, you see, understand your tonality and I've said this to you before, maybe you've forgotten, right? If you get the certification, you definitely shouldn't have forgotten to say it 8,000 times, right? It allows you to apply extra words to whatever you're saying to a word or a phrase so that it gives that word or phrase extra meanings, they get extra words on top of it. So I say to you, you know, how you doing today? You're like, okay, you know, I said to you, hi, Jim, how are you today? You say, you know that I know that I know that you know that you don't really give a shit how I'm doing today versus how you doing today, Jim? You're like, oh, okay, he really wants to know just a little bit of difference. So he really wants to know. Wow. And those are the actual words you hear. Wow, he really wants to know. She really wants to know. I say, now, Jim, the reason for the call today, you're like, wow, this must be important. What's that? It's like some mystery injury involved. See, something just came across my, if I say to Jim, something just came across my desk, it's not the same thing as, Jim, something just came across my desk. It's just not, you don't, you hear additional words. You're like, wow, what, what just came across this with, you can't help yourself. Your mind, your unconscious mind immediately interprets the tonality and adds on the extra words and then it causes your conscious mind to apply meaning. What are those words mean? And remember, your unconscious mind has unlimited power to process. All your conscious mind has very limited power to process. You have that spinning wheel of death in a computer, which is your random access memory, your RAM, that short-term memory of a computer. When it maxes out, you get what? Spinning wheel of death, right? When the long-term memory maxes out, you know what happens? I actually just had that, by the way. I actually maxed out my computer's long-term memory. You know what happened? I had to get it, take it back to the manual. Something happened and it had, it's where it actually allowed me to go past the point with the computer wouldn't operate anymore.
